channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Satono Jasmine Aims for Victory in Key Race
item.description: The article focuses on Satono Jasmine, a horse with a strong record in provincial races, as it prepares to compete in a significant event. The piece provides detailed insights into the horse's previou...

item.description: The article details the courses, fee structures, and application process for Kibabii University College. It highlights the diverse academic departments such as Healthcare, Business, Engineering, and C...

item.title: China's exports rise 27% year-on-year driven by AI demand surge
item.description: China's exports surged by 27% in June 2024 compared to the same period in 2023, driven by robust demand fueled by the rapid expansion of artificial intelligence applications. The customs agency report...

item.title: US and Iran escalate tensions with military strikes and UAE condemnation amid rising oil prices
item.description: The article details escalating tensions between the United States and Iran, with the US launching a five-hour military strike on multiple Iranian targets, including Bushehr, Chah Bahar, Jask, Konarak,...

item.title: US and Iran Escalate Conflict with Strikes and Retaliation
item.description: The United States has intensified its military operations against Iran, launching a third consecutive night of strikes targeting Iranian coastal and maritime facilities. These attacks, part of a broad...

item.title: Russian Opposition Figure Arrested Amid Election Campaign
item.description: Boris Nadezhdin, a prominent Russian critic of President Vladimir Putin and the war in Ukraine, was arrested by Russian authorities on Monday. The 63-year-old activist, who previously attempted to run...

item.title: Day 5 of Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial: Key Testimonies and NBI Findings
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte entered its fifth day with key developments in the proceedings. Zuleika Lopez, a close aide, is set to testify on Article 4, which accuses Duterte ...

item.title: Tanzania's Industrial Growth Recognized at 50th Sabasaba Trade Fair
item.description: The 50th edition of the Dar es Salaam International Trade Fair (Sabasaba) concluded with President Dr Hussein Ali Mwinyi emphasizing Tanzania's industrial progress. During his visit to the GF Pavilion...

item.title: Tinubu Appoints Experienced Leaders to Boost Investment and Industrial Growth

item.title: Corruption Allegations Plague NSW Liberals and Labor in Victoria
item.description: The article explores the growing corruption scandals engulfing both the New South Wales Liberal Party and the Victorian Labor government, highlighting how political parties have become conduits for ve...

item.title: FBI and Pakistan Strengthen Counterterrorism Collaboration
item.description: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) director Kash Patel held a meeting with Pakistan's Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi at the FBI headquarters, expressing appreciation for Pakistan's support...

item.title: England Prepares for Tough ODI Series Against Stronger India Squad
item.description: Ben Duckett, England's cricket opener, acknowledged the challenges of facing a rejuvenated Indian team in the upcoming ODI series. The hosts boast a formidable squad featuring Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohl...

item.title: BFAR Expands Local Salt Production to Reduce Import Dependence
item.description: The Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic Resources (BFAR) announced plans to further expand local salt production after the Philippines' output reached nearly 200,000 metric tons in the first half of 2026....

item.title: UK Police Investigate Possible Terrorism Motive in Politician's Killing
item.description: Ann Widdecombe, a 78-year-old former Conservative minister, was found dead at her home in southwest England. Anti-terrorism police have taken charge of the investigation into her killing, with UK Home...

item.title: National Infrastructure Fund Opens CEO Recruitment with Over 15 Requirements
item.description: Kenya's National Infrastructure Fund (NIF) has launched a competitive recruitment process for the role of Chief Executive Officer, seeking experienced professionals to lead the organization. The posit...

item.title: Springbok Women's Squad Expanded to 49 Players for Upcoming International Season
item.description: Coach Swys de Bruin has announced an expanded 49-player training squad for the South African Springbok Women, set to tackle a demanding international schedule. The squad includes returning players lik...

item.title: Chris Packham’s Evolution Documentary: A Celebration of Nature’s Wonders
item.description: The BBC's new five-part documentary 'Evolution' marks Chris Packham as the natural successor to David Attenborough, blending scientific rigor with poetic wonder. Each episode explores evolutionary mil...

item.title: Rise in Tick-Borne Illnesses Raises Health Concerns Across Canada
item.description: Health officials in Canada are issuing urgent warnings about a significant increase in tick-borne illnesses, with particular focus on anaplasmosis due to its potentially severe health consequences. CB...

item.title: Celebrating 20 Years of the Qinghai–Xizang Railway: Connecting Communities and Promoting Development
item.description: The Qinghai–Xizang Railway, celebrating its 20th anniversary, has become a symbol of connectivity and progress across the Tibetan Plateau. Railway professionals from seven African countries recently e...

item.title: Tanzania Revenue Authority Aims for TZS 41.83 Trillion in 2026/2027
item.description: The Tanzania Revenue Authority (TRA) has set a new revenue collection target of TZS 41.83 trillion for the 2026/2027 financial year, following a successful collection of TZS 37.96 trillion in 2025/202...
